$comment: c65 (D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES, C 6.5.6p9 + 6.3.2.1p3): `pointer - arrayName` is a pointer DIFFERENCE (ptrdiff_t = ELEMENT count) — the array operand decays to Ptr<elem> (C 6.3.2.1p3), then it is `p - q` (c40 ptrSub). Frontier sqlite vdbeSorter `pSorter->iPrev = (u8)(pTask - pSorter->aTask);` (sqlite3.c:107252, a pointer minus a struct-member array). c59's array-decay-in-additive (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-ADDITIVE) decayed an array operand ONLY when the OTHER operand was a SCALAR index, so `p - array` (other operand a Ptr) was left un-decayed → ptrIntArith true → the MIR `combineBinaryOp` `p±n` branch (hir_to_mir.cpp) tried to widen the Array index via mapCast(Array,I64), which returns MirOpcode::Invalid, and addInst(Invalid) std::abort()'d the whole compiler (exit 134, the c64 re-probe crash, NO diagnostic). FIX (NOT a c64 regression — un-masked when c64 cleared the ternary so the compiler reached this construct): (HIR cst_to_hir.cpp combineBinary) an ARRAY operand of pointer Sub decays to Ptr<elem> (covers p-arr, arr-p, arr-arr; `arr - scalarIndex` stays the c59 array-index Ptr form), and ptrSub now reads the decayed lc/rc → the result is I64 (ptrdiff) → the MIR c40 ptrSub path emits PtrToInt+Sub+SDiv(by sizeof(elem)); (MIR hir_to_mir.cpp:1208) a fail-loud guard so any remaining non-int index — e.g. an Enum index, deferred D-CSUBSET-POINTER-ARITH-ENUM-INDEX — emits a clean diagnostic instead of the addInst abort. The 16-byte struct Task element makes the stride DIVISION load-bearing: idx_in_pool(&pool,&aTask[3]) = 48 bytes / 16 = element index 3 (a missing decay/division would yield the byte difference). idx_in_pool (runtime params, not const-folded) 3/0/7 + bare-array e-arr=4 + &arr[k]-arr=3 + the &arr[i]-&arr[j] control=3 → 42. PRESERVE: `p - q` (both pointers, c40) unchanged; `arr - scalarIndex` stays the c59 Ptr result. The decay fires ONLY when the element/pointee types MATCH (a true pointer difference); a MISMATCHED pairing (`int* - char[]`, which gcc rejects) is left un-decayed → it FAILS LOUD (S0008 in a typed context, or the H0009 MIR guard in a pointer context), never a silent miscompile — the audit caught that an unconditional decay turned the c64 abort into a silent wrong answer (exit 8) on `int* - char[]`. The mismatched TWO-pointer `int* - char*` (no array) stays the pre-existing D-CSUBSET-POINTER-DIFF-EDGE-CASES part-2 silent-accept (untouched). RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the HIR Sub array-decay arm → the corpus fails to compile — a clean H0009 'pointer arithmetic: index TypeKind 21 has no widening cast' (21 = Array) via the c65 MIR fail-loud guard; reverting that guard too → the original addInst(MirOpcode::Invalid) std::abort (exit 134, the c64-reprobe crash).
